text:
There was a small village nestled between rolling hills and dense forests. The villagers were peaceful but knew a storm was coming. They called the storm Godfrey.

Godfrey, a great and mysterious storm, hadn't made landfall in centuries. He was said to be capricious, unpredictable, and full of surprises. The villagers prepared their homes with cautionary tales of past storms.

On the eve of the storm, the villagers gathered near the village square, fires crackling against the darkening sky. An elder spoke of ancient times when Godfrey was a welcome guest, bringing rain that grew gardens and crops. Now, they were only feared, and this gathering wasn't about gratitude.

A hush fell over the crowd as they whispered tales of past encounters. Some spoke of his lightning that split wood, others of how his winds roared through the trees till everything was swept clean.

Finally, a brave young boy stepped forward. He had listened intently to the stories and now felt bold enough
